notes: Redesigned ad economy. FREE ads (cost=0): advertisers PAY you  -  upfront_cash is instant sign-on income, income is seasonal. PREMIUM ads (cost>0): you invest for view bonuses (v_flat/v_mult) on top of income. Tradeoff: high upfront vs high seasonal. Premium ads grant views but cost money upfront.

wildcard.desc: An open-ended advertising deal. Specify the target genre  -  the client will brief you on arrival. Sign-on paid immediately.

ads.name: Sludge Cola
ads.desc: Tastes like carbonated regret. Steady revenue from people who know better and keep buying it anyway. Their ad is loud. Your viewers are annoyed.

ads.name: Lizard Insurance
ads.desc: A small CGI reptile explains liability coverage. Beloved by sitcom audiences. The sign-on is generous; keep it on a comedy.

ads.name: Vague Pharma Ad
ads.desc: May cause nausea, drowsiness, and confusion about what the drug actually does. Side effects include profits. Drama audiences eat this up.

ads.name: Local Car Dealership
ads.desc: CRAZY PRICES! MUST GO! WE'RE NOT YELLING, WE'RE JUST EXCITED. Budget-tier but always pays on time. Cash strongly preferred.

ads.name: Lawyer's Finest
ads.desc: HAVE YOU BEEN IN AN ACCIDENT? They heavily imply results in a legally defensible way. Perfect for drama  -  audiences need legal services.

ads.name: CryptoBro Coin(TM)
ads.desc: Guaranteed to 10x or evaporate. High sign-on, high income  -  but SCIFI/REALITY audiences tune out in droves. The check still clears.

ads.name: RivalStream Banner
ads.desc: Advertising for a direct competitor mid-show. Peak indignity. Peak revenue. The sign-on is enormous. Your viewers will hate it.

ads.name: Midnight Mattress King
ads.desc: Suspiciously good prices. Van parked outside. Sleep great on a mattress of unclear origin. Reliable, if unexciting.

ads.name: BargainShield Compare
ads.desc: A talking box with a face explains savings. Somehow more relatable than most human characters on TV. Works best on light programming.

ads.name: Tailgate Waffles
ads.desc: A waffle truck outside the stadium that sells out by halftime. Enormous loyalty among sports fans. High upfront, high seasonal.

ads.name: Loyalty Card Commercial
ads.condition_text: Always: +$9/season. Sign-on $3. Low upfront, steady seasonal.
ads.desc: Sign up today, forget to cancel tomorrow. Modest sign-on but pays reliably every season  -  ideal for a long-running show.

ads.name: Cola Hill Singalong
ads.desc: A famous hilltop jingle for a fizzy drink. Harmonies optional, refreshment mandatory. Works on any show  -  the jingle is universal.

ads.name: Breaking News Alert
ads.desc: Your ad appears immediately after every BREAKING NEWS chyron. Estimated frequency: every 4 minutes. Enormous on NEWS.

ads.name: Cut-Rate Lizard Clone
ads.desc: A cheaper reptile mascot. Legally distinct from the other one. SITCOM audiences still buy it. Strong seasonal on comedy.

ads.name: Network Self-Promo
ads.condition_text: Always: Views x1.2, Income -$2/season. PREMIUM: invest for views.
ads.desc: Promote yourself to promote yourself. You pay upfront, lose a little income, but gain 20% more viewers. Works best on already-large audiences.

ads.name: Streaming Pivot Banner
ads.condition_text: Always: Views x1.1, Income -$2/season. PREMIUM: budget view boost.
ads.desc: A smaller view investment. Corporate synergy  -  you pay, they amplify your audience by 10%. Better ROI on high-base-view shows.

ads.name: Stretch Seal
ads.desc: Seals boats, trucks, and questionable life decisions. Phil Swift demands placement fees  -  but adds 25 views on REALITY. Worth the cost.

ads.name: Turbo Energy Drink
ads.desc: Sponsors everything with wheels. Premium placement required  -  but brings 20 extra viewers AND solid income on SPORTS. Strong value on SPORTS shows.

ads.name: Meme Energy Drink
ads.desc: Fueled by chaos, powered by hashtags. Modest income but 30 flat views on light programming. Good for boosting viewer counts cheaply.

ads.name: Binge Box Subscription
ads.desc: A monthly subscription service. Genre fans binge your show AND subscribe. Invest to gain both income and views on prestige programming.

ads.name: Crypto For Pets(TM)
ads.desc: Your dog now has a wallet address. SCIFI audiences inexplicably love it  -  22 bonus views AND solid income. Premium placement fee required.

ads.name: Fruit 1984
ads.desc: A dystopian big-game spot with a flying hammer. The highest placement cost in the business  -  but 30 extra views on prestige genres. A prestige move.

ads.name: Chef's Secret Sauce(TM)
ads.desc: A condiment brand with more secrets than the recipe on the label. Enormous on COOKING; generic elsewhere. The sign-on arrives in cash.

ads.name: Discount Gym Pass
ads.desc: January memberships. March cancellations. The cycle is reliable and the revenue is steady on physical programming.

ads.name: RecipeBox(TM) Delivery
ads.desc: Pre-portioned ingredients delivered to your door. The ad budget is larger than most cooking show production budgets.

ads.name: BetZone Sports(TM)
ads.desc: The odds are always in their favour and never yours. Enormous seasonal income on SPORTS. The sign-on bonus is substantial.

ads.name: FreshMart Weekly
ads.desc: A grocery chain with an extremely cheerful tomato mascot. Works best adjacent to food and lifestyle programming.

ads.name: Prestige Cookware(TM)
ads.desc: Pans that cost more than most appliances. COOKING audiences buy three. Premium placement required for the full bonus.

ads.name: True Crime Pod Crossover
ads.desc: Fifteen minutes about a murder, then an ad. The crossover is seamless. Prestige drama and news audiences love a good true crime hook.

ads.name: TechBro Launchpad(TM)
ads.desc: A startup that pivoted four times and is now a television sponsor. SCIFI audiences inexplicably trust them. Strong premium ROI.

ads.name: Chronos Luxury Watches
ads.condition_text: Always: +$30 income, but Views x0.85 (snobby, alienating)
ads.desc: An ad so opulent it makes your audience feel poor and slightly resentful. The cheque clears beautifully; the viewers drift.

ads.name: RivalStream Takeover
ads.condition_text: Always: big +$45 upfront, then Views x0.8 ongoing
ads.desc: A competitor pays a fortune up front to advertise their own platform on your air. The cash is glorious. The slow viewer exodus, less so.

ads.name: Synergy Ad Consortium
ads.condition_text: If sharing with other ads: +$10 income per attached ad
ads.desc: A media buyer that pays bulk rates — the more sponsors already crowding the show, the sweeter the deal it cuts you.

ads.name: Maison Prestige
ads.condition_text: On DRAMA: +$22 income & Views x1.05. Off-genre: +$6
ads.desc: A heritage fashion house that only wants to be seen beside prestige drama. Put it where it belongs and it pays a premium and lends class.

ads.name: Guerrilla Viral Campaign
ads.condition_text: Always: +90 views, but -$2 income (you foot the buzz bill)
ads.desc: A stunt campaign engineered for clicks, not cash. It drags eyeballs in by the thousand and quietly bills you for the privilege.

ads.name: 3AM Miracle Infomercial
ads.desc: A relentless direct-response spot that prints money in the graveyard reruns of an established show. Set-and-forget revenue.

ads.name: Overdrive Energy
ads.desc: A garishly loud sponsor that pays well and pulls a few curious viewers, even as it grates on everyone else. A noisy net win.

ads.name: Civic Trust Bank
ads.condition_text: On non-REALITY shows: +$20 income. On reality: +$5 (brand-unsafe)
ads.desc: A buttoned-up institution that will pay handsomely to sit beside respectable programming — and flees the chaos of reality TV.
